title: Scene II — The Chorus (First Appearance)

segments.ssml: <speak><prosody rate="slow"><emphasis level="moderate">Someone else,</emphasis> <break time="600ms"/> not far from a morally right human consensus, <break time="600ms"/> will be chosen <break time="600ms"/> to hard-wire loving thoughts <break time="600ms"/> into our brains.</prosody></speak>
segments.director_note: First hearing of the refrain. Landed monotone-chanted rather than sung; the line is not explained — it arrives. Emphasis on the two opening words. Each | is a 600ms sacred pause.
Hold the final word 'brains' by letting the silence that follows inherit the reverb tail. Do not fill.

overall_notes: First Chorus appearance. The congregation fused into a single voice — rendered here by layering three female voices with small timing offsets (ensemble defined in voice-map.ts). Heavy reverb and slight pitch-down would land in post-processing if available; for now, the natural pitch diversity of the ensemble carries most of the choral character.
